revision.comment: Reverted edits by [[Special:Contribs/2601:3C6:100:C660:74D0:CFC0:B1F1:3709|2601:3C6:100:C660:74D0:CFC0:B1F1:3709]] ([[User talk:2601:3C6:100:C660:74D0:CFC0:B1F1:3709|talk]]) to last version by JohnFromPinckney
revision.text: URLs must begin with a supported [[URI scheme]]. http:// and https:// will be supported by all browsers; however, ftp://, gopher://, irc://, ircs://, mailto: and news: may require a plug-in or an external application and should normally be avoided. [[IPv6]] host-names are currently not supported. If URLs in [[Wikipedia:Citation_templates#Examples|citation template parameters]] contain certain characters, then they will not display and link correctly. Those characters need to be [[percent-encoded]]. For example, a space must be replaced by %20. To encode the URL, replace the following characters with: {| class="wikitable" !scope="row"| Character !scope="col"| space !scope="col"| " !scope="col"| ' !scope="col"| < !scope="col"| > !scope="col"| [ !scope="col"| ] !scope="col"| { !scope="col"| | !scope="col"| } |- !scope="row"| Encoding | %20 || %22 || %27 || %3C || %3E || %5B || %5D || %7B || %7C || %7D |} Single apostrophes do not need to be encoded; however, unencoded multiples will be parsed as italic or bold markup. Single curly closing braces also do not need to be encoded; however, an unencoded pair will be parsed as the double closing braces for the template transclusion. {{documentation}}

URLs must begin with a supported [[URI scheme]]. http:// and https:// will be supported by all browsers; however, ftp://, gopher://, irc://, ircs://, mailto: and news: may require a plug-in or an external application and should normally be avoided. [[IPv6]] host-names are currently not supported. If URLs in [[Wikipedia:Citation_templates#Examples|citation template parameters]] contain certain characters, then they will not display and link correctly. Those characters need to be [[percent-encoded]]. For example, a space must be replaced by %20. To encode the URL, replace the following characters with: {| class="wikitable" !scope="row"| Character !scope="col"| space !scope="col"| " !scope="col"| ' !scope="col"| < !scope="col"| > !scope="col"| [ !scope="col"| ] !scope="col"| { !scope="col"| | !scope="col"| } |- !scope="row"| Encoding | %20 || %22 || %27 || %3C || %3E || %5B || %5D || %7B || %7C || %7D |} Single apostrophes do not need to be encoded; however, unencoded multiples will be parsed as italic or bold markup. Single curly closing braces also do not need to be encoded; however, an unencoded pair will be parsed as the double closing braces for the template transclusion. {{documentation}}

revision.comment: Reverted edits by [[Special:Contribs/2601:3C6:100:C660:74D0:CFC0:B1F1:3709|2601:3C6:100:C660:74D0:CFC0:B1F1:3709]] ([[User talk:2601:3C6:100:C660:74D0:CFC0:B1F1:3709|talk]]) to last version by JohnFromPinckney
revision.text: URLs must begin with a supported [[URI scheme]]. http:// and https:// will be supported by all browsers; however, ftp://, gopher://, irc://, ircs://, mailto: and news: may require a plug-in or an external application and should normally be avoided. [[IPv6]] host-names are currently not supported. If URLs in [[Wikipedia:Citation_templates#Examples|citation template parameters]] contain certain characters, then they will not display and link correctly. Those characters need to be [[percent-encoded]]. For example, a space must be replaced by %20. To encode the URL, replace the following characters with: {| class="wikitable" !scope="row"| Character !scope="col"| space !scope="col"| " !scope="col"| ' !scope="col"| < !scope="col"| > !scope="col"| [ !scope="col"| ] !scope="col"| { !scope="col"| | !scope="col"| } |- !scope="row"| Encoding | %20 || %22 || %27 || %3C || %3E || %5B || %5D || %7B || %7C || %7D |} Single apostrophes do not need to be encoded; however, unencoded multiples will be parsed as italic or bold markup. Single curly closing braces also do not need to be encoded; however, an unencoded pair will be parsed as the double closing braces for the template transclusion. {{documentation}}

revision.comment: Reverted edits by [[Special:Contribs/2601:3C6:100:C660:74D0:CFC0:B1F1:3709|2601:3C6:100:C660:74D0:CFC0:B1F1:3709]] ([[User talk:2601:3C6:100:C660:74D0:CFC0:B1F1:3709|talk]]) to last version by JohnFromPinckney
revision.text: URLs must begin with a supported [[URI scheme]]. http:// and https:// will be supported by all browsers; however, ftp://, gopher://, irc://, ircs://, mailto: and news: may require a plug-in or an external application and should normally be avoided. [[IPv6]] host-names are currently not supported. If URLs in [[Wikipedia:Citation_templates#Examples|citation template parameters]] contain certain characters, then they will not display and link correctly. Those characters need to be [[percent-encoded]]. For example, a space must be replaced by %20. To encode the URL, replace the following characters with: {| class="wikitable" !scope="row"| Character !scope="col"| space !scope="col"| " !scope="col"| ' !scope="col"| < !scope="col"| > !scope="col"| [ !scope="col"| ] !scope="col"| { !scope="col"| | !scope="col"| } |- !scope="row"| Encoding | %20 || %22 || %27 || %3C || %3E || %5B || %5D || %7B || %7C || %7D |} Single apostrophes do not need to be encoded; however, unencoded multiples will be parsed as italic or bold markup. Single curly closing braces also do not need to be encoded; however, an unencoded pair will be parsed as the double closing braces for the template transclusion. {{documentation}}

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: I hope this manages to go through
revision.text: URLs must begin with a supported [[URI scheme]]. http:// and https:// will be supported by all browsers; however, ftp://, gopher://, irc://, ircs://, mailto: and news: may require a plug-in or an external application and should normally be avoided. [[IPv6]] host-names are currently not supported. If URLs in [[Wikipedia:Citation_templates#Examples|citation template parameters]] contain certain characters, then they will not display and link correctly. Those characters need to be [[percent-encoded]]. For example, a space must be replaced by %20. To encode the URL, replace the following characters with: {| class="wikitable" !scope="row"| Character !scope="col"| space !scope="col"| " !scope="col"| ' !scope="col"| < !scope="col"| > !scope="col"| [ !scope="col"| ] !scope="col"| { !scope="col"| | !scope="col"| } |- !scope="row"| Encoding | %20 || %22 || %27 || %3C || %3E || %5B || %5D || %7B || %7C || %7D |} Single apostrophes do not need to be encoded; however, unencoded multiples will be parsed as italic or bold markup. Single curly closing braces also do not need to be encoded; however, an unencoded pair will be parsed as the double closing braces for the template transclusion. {{documentation}}

revision.comment: Reverted edits by [[Special:Contribs/2601:3C6:100:C660:74D0:CFC0:B1F1:3709|2601:3C6:100:C660:74D0:CFC0:B1F1:3709]] ([[User talk:2601:3C6:100:C660:74D0:CFC0:B1F1:3709|talk]]) to last version by JohnFromPinckney
revision.text: URLs must begin with a supported [[URI scheme]]. http:// and https:// will be supported by all browsers; however, ftp://, gopher://, irc://, ircs://, mailto: and news: may require a plug-in or an external application and should normally be avoided. [[IPv6]] host-names are currently not supported. If URLs in [[Wikipedia:Citation_templates#Examples|citation template parameters]] contain certain characters, then they will not display and link correctly. Those characters need to be [[percent-encoded]]. For example, a space must be replaced by %20. To encode the URL, replace the following characters with: {| class="wikitable" !scope="row"| Character !scope="col"| space !scope="col"| " !scope="col"| ' !scope="col"| < !scope="col"| > !scope="col"| [ !scope="col"| ] !scope="col"| { !scope="col"| | !scope="col"| } |- !scope="row"| Encoding | %20 || %22 || %27 || %3C || %3E || %5B || %5D || %7B || %7C || %7D |} Single apostrophes do not need to be encoded; however, unencoded multiples will be parsed as italic or bold markup. Single curly closing braces also do not need to be encoded; however, an unencoded pair will be parsed as the double closing braces for the template transclusion. {{documentation}}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text: URLs must begin with a supported [[URI scheme]]. http:// and https:// will be supported by all browsers; however, ftp://, gopher://, irc://, ircs://, mailto: and news: may require a plug-in or an external application and should normally be avoided. [[:en:IPv6|IPv6]] host-names are currently not supported. If URLs in [[Wikipedia:Citation_templates#Examples|citation template parameters]] contain certain characters, then they will not display and link correctly. Those characters need to be [[percent-encoded]]. For example, a space must be replaced by %20. To encode the URL, replace the following characters with: {| class="wikitable" !scope="row"| Character !scope="col"| space !scope="col"| " !scope="col"| ' !scope="col"| < !scope="col"| > !scope="col"| [ !scope="col"| ] !scope="col"| { !scope="col"| | !scope="col"| } |- !scope="row"| Encoding | %20 || %22 || %27 || %3C || %3E || %5B || %5D || %7B || %7C || %7D |} Single apostrophes do not need to be encoded; however, unencoded multiples will be parsed as italic or bold markup. Single curly closing braces also do not need to be encoded; however, an unencoded pair will be parsed as the double closing braces for the template transclusion. {{documentation}}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text: URLs must begin with a supported [[URI scheme]]. http:// and https:// will be supported by all browsers; however, ftp://, gopher://, irc://, ircs://, mailto: and news: may require a plug-in or an external application and should normally be avoided. [[:en:IPv6|IPv6]] host-names are currently not supported. If URLs in [[Wikipedia:Citation_templates#Examples|citation template parameters]] contain certain characters, then they will not display and link correctly. Those characters need to be [[:en:Percent-encoded|Percent-encoded]]. For example, a space must be replaced by %20. To encode the URL, replace the following characters with: {| class="wikitable" !scope="row"| Character !scope="col"| space !scope="col"| " !scope="col"| ' !scope="col"| < !scope="col"| > !scope="col"| [ !scope="col"| ] !scope="col"| { !scope="col"| | !scope="col"| } |- !scope="row"| Encoding | %20 || %22 || %27 || %3C || %3E || %5B || %5D || %7B || %7C || %7D |} Single apostrophes do not need to be encoded; however, unencoded multiples will be parsed as italic or bold markup. Single curly closing braces also do not need to be encoded; however, an unencoded pair will be parsed as the double closing braces for the template transclusion. {{documentation}}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text: URLs must begin with a supported [[:en:URI scheme|URI scheme]]. http:// and https:// will be supported by all browsers; however, ftp://, gopher://, irc://, ircs://, mailto: and news: may require a plug-in or an external application and should normally be avoided. [[:en:IPv6|IPv6]] host-names are currently not supported. If URLs in [[Wikipedia:Citation_templates#Examples|citation template parameters]] contain certain characters, then they will not display and link correctly. Those characters need to be [[:en:Percent-encoded|Percent-encoded]]. For example, a space must be replaced by %20. To encode the URL, replace the following characters with: {| class="wikitable" !scope="row"| Character !scope="col"| space !scope="col"| " !scope="col"| ' !scope="col"| < !scope="col"| > !scope="col"| [ !scope="col"| ] !scope="col"| { !scope="col"| | !scope="col"| } |- !scope="row"| Encoding | %20 || %22 || %27 || %3C || %3E || %5B || %5D || %7B || %7C || %7D |} Single apostrophes do not need to be encoded; however, unencoded multiples will be parsed as italic or bold markup. Single curly closing braces also do not need to be encoded; however, an unencoded pair will be parsed as the double closing braces for the template transclusion. {{documentation}}

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text: URLs must begin with a supported [[:en:URI scheme|URI scheme]]. http:// and https:// will be supported by all browsers; however, ftp://, gopher://, irc://, ircs://, mailto: and news: may require a plug-in or an external application and should normally be avoided. [[:en:IPv6|IPv6]] host-names are currently not supported. If URLs in [[Wikipedia:Citation_templates#Examples|citation template parameters]] contain certain characters, then they will not display and link correctly. Those characters need to be [[:en:Percent-encoded|Percent-encoded]]. For example, a space must be replaced by %20. To encode the URL, replace the following characters with: {| class="wikitable" !scope="row"| Character !scope="col"| space !scope="col"| " !scope="col"| ' !scope="col"| < !scope="col"| > !scope="col"| [ !scope="col"| ] !scope="col"| { !scope="col"| | !scope="col"| } |- !scope="row"| Encoding | %20 || %22 || %27 || %3C || %3E || %5B || %5D || %7B || %7C || %7D |} Single apostrophes do not need to be encoded; however, unencoded multiples will be parsed as italic or bold markup. Single curly closing braces also do not need to be encoded; however, an unencoded pair will be parsed as the double closing braces for the template transclusion. {{documentation}} [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
revision.text: URLs must begin with a supported [[:en:URI scheme|URI scheme]]. http:// and https:// will be supported by all browsers; however, ftp://, gopher://, irc://, ircs://, mailto: and news: may require a plug-in or an external application and should normally be avoided. [[:en:IPv6|IPv6]] host-names are currently not supported. If URLs in [[Wikipedia:Citation_templates#Examples|citation template parameters]] contain certain characters, then they will not display and link correctly. Those characters need to be [[:en:Percent-encoded|Percent-encoded]]. For example, a space must be replaced by %20. To encode the URL, replace the following characters with: {| class="wikitable" !scope="row"| Character !scope="col"| space !scope="col"| " !scope="col"| ' !scope="col"| < !scope="col"| > !scope="col"| [ !scope="col"| ] !scope="col"| { !scope="col"| | !scope="col"| } |- !scope="row"| Encoding | %20 || %22 || %27 || %3C || %3E || %5B || %5D || %7B || %7C || %7D |} Single apostrophes do not need to be encoded; however, unencoded multiples will be parsed as italic or bold markup. Single curly closing braces also do not need to be encoded; however, an unencoded pair will be parsed as the double closing braces for the template transclusion. {{documentation}}

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text: URLs must begin with a supported [[:en:URI scheme|URI scheme]]. http:// and https:// will be supported by all browsers; however, ftp://, gopher://, irc://, ircs://, mailto: and news: may require a plug-in or an external application and should normally be avoided. [[:en:IPv6|IPv6]] host-names are currently not supported. If URLs in [[Wikipedia:Citation_templates#Examples|citation template parameters]] contain certain characters, then they will not display and link correctly. Those characters need to be [[:en:Percent-encoded|Percent-encoded]]. For example, a space must be replaced by %20. To encode the URL, replace the following characters with: {| class="wikitable" !scope="row"| Character !scope="col"| space !scope="col"| " !scope="col"| ' !scope="col"| < !scope="col"| > !scope="col"| [ !scope="col"| ] !scope="col"| { !scope="col"| | !scope="col"| } |- !scope="row"| Encoding | %20 || %22 || %27 || %3C || %3E || %5B || %5D || %7B || %7C || %7D |} Single apostrophes do not need to be encoded; however, unencoded multiples will be parsed as italic or bold markup. Single curly closing braces also do not need to be encoded; however, an unencoded pair will be parsed as the double closing braces for the template transclusion. {{documentation}} [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 1 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23513454770|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: URLs must begin with a supported [[:en:URI scheme|URI scheme]]. http:// and https:// will be supported by all browsers; however, ftp://, gopher://, irc://, ircs://, mailto: and news: may require a plug-in or an external application and should normally be avoided. [[:en:IPv6|IPv6]] host-names are currently not supported. If URLs in [[Wikipedia:Citation_templates#Examples|citation template parameters]] contain certain characters, then they will not display and link correctly. Those characters need to be [[:en:Percent-encoded|Percent-encoded]]. For example, a space must be replaced by %20. To encode the URL, replace the following characters with: {| class="wikitable" !scope="row"| Character !scope="col"| space !scope="col"| " !scope="col"| ' !scope="col"| < !scope="col"| > !scope="col"| [ !scope="col"| ] !scope="col"| { !scope="col"| | !scope="col"| } |- !scope="row"| Encoding | %20 || %22 || %27 || %3C || %3E || %5B || %5D || %7B || %7C || %7D |} Single apostrophes do not need to be encoded; however, unencoded multiples will be parsed as italic or bold markup. Single curly closing braces also do not need to be encoded; however, an unencoded pair will be parsed as the double closing braces for the template transclusion. {{documentation}} [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

URLs must begin with a supported [[:en:URI scheme|URI scheme]]. http:// and https:// will be supported by all browsers; however, ftp://, gopher://, irc://, ircs://, mailto: and news: may require a plug-in or an external application and should normally be avoided. [[:en:IPv6|IPv6]] host-names are currently not supported. If URLs in [[Wikipedia:Citation_templates#Examples|citation template parameters]] contain certain characters, then they will not display and link correctly. Those characters need to be [[:en:Percent-encoded|Percent-encoded]]. For example, a space must be replaced by %20. To encode the URL, replace the following characters with: {| class="wikitable" !scope="row"| Character !scope="col"| space !scope="col"| " !scope="col"| ' !scope="col"| < !scope="col"| > !scope="col"| [ !scope="col"| ] !scope="col"| { !scope="col"| | !scope="col"| } |- !scope="row"| Encoding | %20 || %22 || %27 || %3C || %3E || %5B || %5D || %7B || %7C || %7D |} Single apostrophes do not need to be encoded; however, unencoded multiples will be parsed as italic or bold markup. Single curly closing braces also do not need to be encoded; however, an unencoded pair will be parsed as the double closing braces for the template transclusion. {{documentation}} [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
